translations.literal: Moreover, if the world has two Emperors, does Your Majesty think the world will honor Qi?
translations.idiomatic: "Furthermore, if two Emperors are established, does Your Majesty think the world will honor Qi?"

sentences.zh: 尊秦乎？」

translations.literal: Or honor Qin?
translations.idiomatic: "Or will it honor Qin?"

sentences.zh: 王曰：「尊秦。」

translations.literal: The King said: 'Honor Qin.'
translations.idiomatic: The King replied: "It will honor Qin."

sentences.zh: 曰：「釋帝，天下愛齊乎？

translations.literal: He said: 'If you renounce the title of Emperor, will the world love Qi?'
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai continued: "And if you renounce the title, will the world love Qi?"

sentences.zh: 愛秦乎？」

translations.literal: Or love Qin?
translations.idiomatic: "Or love Qin?"

sentences.zh: 王曰：「愛齊而憎秦。」

translations.literal: The King said: 'Love Qi and hate Qin.'
translations.idiomatic: The King answered: "They will love Qi and despise Qin."

sentences.zh: 曰：「兩帝立約伐趙，孰與伐桀宋之利？」

translations.literal: He said: 'If the two Emperors make a pact to attack Zhao, how does that compare in advantage to attacking the tyrannical Song?'
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai pressed further: "If the two Emperors make a pact to attack Zhao, would that be as advantageous as attacking the tyrannical ruler of Song?"

sentences.zh: 王曰：「伐桀宋利。」

translations.literal: The King said: 'Attacking the tyrannical Song is more advantageous.'
translations.idiomatic: The King answered: "Attacking Song would be more advantageous."

sentences.zh: 對曰：「夫約鈞，然與秦為帝而天下獨尊秦而輕齊，釋帝則天下愛齊而憎秦，伐趙不如伐桀宋之利，故願王明釋帝以收天下，倍約賓秦，無爭重，而王以其閒舉宋。

translations.literal: He replied: 'The treaties are balanced, yet sharing the title of Emperor with Qin causes the world to honor only Qin and slight Qi. Renouncing the title makes the world love Qi and hate Qin. Attacking Zhao is not as profitable as attacking the tyrannical Song. Therefore I urge Your Majesty to openly renounce the title to win the world, break the pact and treat Qin as a guest, do not contend for weight, and in the interim take Song.'
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai replied: "The balance of the treaties notwithstanding, sharing the imperial title with Qin merely causes the world to honor Qin and slight Qi. Renouncing it, on the other hand, wins the world's love for Qi and its hatred of Qin. Attacking Zhao is less profitable than destroying the tyrannical ruler of Song. I urge Your Majesty, therefore, to openly renounce the title in order to win all under Heaven, break the pact and keep Qin at a polite distance, avoid a direct contest for primacy, and in the interim seize the state of Song.

sentences.zh: 夫有宋，衛之陽地危；

translations.literal: If you possess Song, the northern lands of Wei are endangered.
translations.idiomatic: "Once you hold Song, the northern territories of Wei are at risk."

sentences.zh: 有濟西，趙之阿東國危；

translations.literal: If you hold territory west of the Ji River, the eastern state of Zhao at E is endangered.
translations.idiomatic: "Hold the lands west of the Ji River, and Zhao's eastern territories near E are imperiled."

sentences.zh: 有淮北，楚之東國危；

translations.literal: If you hold territory north of the Huai River, Chu's eastern territories are endangered.
translations.idiomatic: "Hold the lands north of the Huai River, and Chu's eastern domains are threatened."

sentences.zh: 有陶、平陸，梁門不開。

translations.literal: If you hold Tao and Pinglu, the gate of Liang cannot open.
translations.idiomatic: "Hold Tao and Pinglu, and the gateway to Liang is sealed shut."

sentences.zh: 釋帝而貸之以伐桀宋之事，國重而名尊，燕楚所以形服，天下莫敢不聽，此湯武之舉也。

translations.literal: Renounce the title and trade it for the conquest of the tyrannical Song. The state grows powerful and its name honored; Yan and Chu will submit in form; none under Heaven will dare disobey. This is an enterprise worthy of Tang and Wu.
translations.idiomatic: "Renounce the title and trade it for the conquest of Song. Your state shall grow mighty and your name honored. Yan and Chu will be brought to submission, and none under Heaven will dare disobey. This is an enterprise worthy of Tang and Wu."

sentences.zh: 敬秦以為名，而後使天下憎之，此所謂以卑為尊者也。

translations.literal: Show respect to Qin in name, then cause the world to resent it — this is what is called using humility to achieve honor.
translations.idiomatic: "Show deference to Qin in name, then turn the world against it — this is what is called rising to greatness through humility."

sentences.zh: 願王孰慮之。」

translations.literal: I urge Your Majesty to consider this carefully.
translations.idiomatic: "I urge Your Majesty to consider this carefully."

sentences.zh: 於是齊去帝復為王，秦亦去帝位。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qi renounced the title of Emperor and reverted to being a king. Qin also relinquished the title of Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: And so Qi renounced the title of Emperor and reverted to the title of king. Qin likewise relinquished its imperial title.

sentences.zh: 三十八年，伐宋。

translations.literal: In the thirty-eighth year, they attacked Song.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirty-eighth year, Qi attacked Song.

sentences.zh: 秦昭王怒曰：「吾愛宋與愛新城、陽晉同。

translations.literal: King Zhao of Qin was angry, saying: 'My affection for Song is the same as my affection for Xincheng and Yangjin.'
translations.idiomatic: King Zhao of Qin was incensed: "My concern for Song is no less than my concern for Xincheng and Yangjin."

sentences.zh: 韓聶與吾友也，而攻吾所愛，何也？」

translations.literal: Han Nie is my friend, yet he attacks what I care for. Why?
translations.idiomatic: "Han Nie is supposed to be my friend, yet he attacks what I hold dear. Why?"

sentences.zh: 蘇代為齊謂秦王曰：「韓聶之攻宋，所以為王也。

translations.literal: Su Dai, speaking on behalf of Qi, told the King of Qin: 'Han Nie's attack on Song is actually for Your Majesty's benefit.'
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai, speaking on Qi's behalf, addressed the King of Qin: "Han Nie's attack on Song is in fact being carried out for Your Majesty's benefit."

sentences.zh: 齊彊，輔之以宋，楚魏必恐，恐必西事秦，是王不煩一兵，不傷一士，無事而割安邑也，此韓聶之所禱於王也。」

translations.literal: Qi, being strong and supported by Song, will cause Chu and Wei to be fearful. Fearful, they will surely turn west to serve Qin. Your Majesty need not trouble a single soldier or harm a single officer, yet gain Anyi without effort. This is what Han Nie prays for on Your Majesty's behalf.
translations.idiomatic: "A powerful Qi, supplemented by Song, will terrify Chu and Wei. In their terror, they will inevitably turn westward to serve Qin. Thus Your Majesty, without deploying a single soldier or losing a single officer, shall acquire Anyi effortlessly. This is the very outcome that Han Nie prays for on Your Majesty's behalf."

sentences.zh: 秦王曰：「吾患齊之難知。

translations.literal: The King of Qin said: 'My concern is that Qi's intentions are hard to fathom.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin said: "My concern is that Qi's true intentions are impossible to read."

sentences.zh: 一從一衡，其說何也？」

translations.literal: One day it follows the Vertical Alliance, the next the Horizontal — what is its strategy?
translations.idiomatic: "One day it joins the Vertical Alliance, the next the Horizontal. What strategy is it pursuing?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「天下國令齊可知乎？

translations.literal: He replied: 'Can any state under Heaven truly understand Qi?'
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai replied: "Can any state under Heaven truly know Qi's mind?"

sentences.zh: 齊以攻宋，其知事秦以萬乘之國自輔，不西事秦則宋治不安。

translations.literal: Qi, in attacking Song, knows it must serve Qin and use a state of ten thousand chariots to support itself. If it does not serve Qin in the west, Song cannot be pacified.
translations.idiomatic: "Qi's attack on Song makes clear that it understands the need to keep Qin as an ally and use a great power as its support. If Qi does not maintain its western ties to Qin, Song cannot be pacified."

sentences.zh: 中國白頭游敖之士皆積智欲離齊秦之交，伏式結軼西馳者，未有一人言善齊者也，伏式結軼東馳者，未有一人言善秦者也。

translations.literal: The white-haired wandering scholars of the Central States all accumulate their wisdom to break apart the alliance of Qi and Qin. Among those bowing over their carriage rails and racing west, not one speaks well of Qi; among those bowing and racing east, not one speaks well of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: "The gray-haired traveling counselors of the Central States all devote their wits to breaking apart the alliance between Qi and Qin. Among those who ride westward, bowing over their carriage rails, not a single one speaks well of Qi. Among those who ride eastward, not a single one speaks well of Qin."

sentences.zh: 何則？

translations.literal: Why is this?
translations.idiomatic: "Why is this?"

sentences.zh: 皆不欲齊秦之合也。

translations.literal: They all do not want Qi and Qin to unite.
translations.idiomatic: "Because none of them want Qi and Qin to be united."

sentences.zh: 何晉楚之智而齊秦之愚也！

translations.literal: How wise are Jin and Chu, and how foolish are Qi and Qin!
translations.idiomatic: "How shrewd are Jin and Chu, and how foolish would Qi and Qin be to fall for this!"

sentences.zh: 晉楚合必議齊秦，齊秦合必圖晉楚，請以此決事。」

translations.literal: If Jin and Chu unite, they will plot against Qi and Qin. If Qi and Qin unite, they will plot against Jin and Chu. I ask that this principle be used to decide matters.
translations.idiomatic: "When Jin and Chu unite, they plot against Qi and Qin. When Qi and Qin unite, they dominate Jin and Chu. I urge Your Majesty to base your decision on this principle."

sentences.zh: 秦王曰：「諾。」

translations.literal: The King of Qin said: 'Agreed.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin said: "Very well."

sentences.zh: 於是齊遂伐宋，宋王出亡，死於溫。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qi attacked Song. The King of Song fled and died at Wen.
translations.idiomatic: And so Qi proceeded to conquer Song. The King of Song fled and died in exile at Wen.

sentences.zh: 齊南割楚之淮北，西侵三晉，欲以并周室，為天子。

translations.literal: Qi seized the lands north of the Huai from Chu to the south, encroached upon the Three Jin to the west, and intended to annex the Zhou royal domain and become Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Qi then seized the Huai River's northern territories from Chu, encroached upon the Three Jin to the west, and harbored ambitions of annexing the Zhou royal domain to become Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 泗上諸侯鄒魯之君皆稱臣，諸侯恐懼。

translations.literal: The lords along the Si River and the rulers of Zou and Lu all declared themselves Qi's vassals. The feudal lords were terrified.
translations.idiomatic: The lords along the Si River and the rulers of Zou and Lu all declared themselves Qi's vassals, and the feudal lords were seized with dread.

sentences.zh: 三十九年，秦來伐，拔我列城九。

translations.literal: In the thirty-ninth year, Qin attacked and captured nine of our walled cities.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirty-ninth year, Qin attacked and captured nine of Qi's walled cities.

sentences.zh: 四十年，燕、秦、楚、三晉合謀，各出銳師以伐，敗我濟西。

translations.literal: In the fortieth year, Yan, Qin, Chu, and the Three Jin conspired together, each sending elite troops to attack. They defeated us west of the Ji River.
translations.idiomatic: In the fortieth year, Yan, Qin, Chu, and the Three Jin states conspired together and each sent crack troops to attack Qi. They defeated Qi's forces west of the Ji River.

sentences.zh: 王解而卻。

translations.literal: The king's forces dispersed and retreated.
translations.idiomatic: The king's army broke apart and retreated.

sentences.zh: 燕將樂毅遂入臨淄，盡取齊之寶藏器。

translations.literal: The Yan general Yue Yi then entered Linzi and seized all of Qi's treasures and precious objects.
translations.idiomatic: The Yan general Yue Yi then marched into the capital Linzi and plundered all of Qi's treasures and precious vessels.

sentences.zh: 湣王出亡，之衛。

translations.literal: King Min fled and went to Wei.
translations.idiomatic: King Min fled the capital and made his way to the state of Wei.

sentences.zh: 衛君辟宮捨之，稱臣而共具。

translations.literal: The lord of Wei vacated his palace and lodged him, declared himself a vassal, and provided for him.
translations.idiomatic: The lord of Wei vacated his own palace for the king, declared himself a vassal, and furnished him with provisions.

sentences.zh: 湣王不遜，衛人侵之。

translations.literal: King Min was arrogant, and the people of Wei turned against him.
translations.idiomatic: But King Min behaved arrogantly, and the people of Wei turned hostile.

sentences.zh: 湣王去，走鄒、魯，有驕色，鄒、魯君弗內，遂走莒。

translations.literal: King Min left and fled to Zou and Lu, but showed an arrogant demeanor. The rulers of Zou and Lu refused to admit him, so he fled to Ju.
translations.idiomatic: King Min departed and fled to Zou and Lu, but his arrogant bearing offended the rulers there, and they refused to take him in. He was forced to flee to Ju.

sentences.zh: 楚使淖齒將兵救齊，因相齊湣王。

translations.literal: Chu sent Nao Chi to lead troops to rescue Qi, and he became chief minister to King Min of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Chu sent the general Nao Chi to lead an army to Qi's rescue, and Nao Chi was subsequently made chief minister to King Min.

sentences.zh: 淖齒遂殺湣王而與燕共分齊之侵地鹵器。

translations.literal: Nao Chi then killed King Min and together with Yan divided Qi's conquered territories and plundered treasures.
translations.idiomatic: Nao Chi then murdered King Min and joined with Yan in dividing Qi's conquered lands and plundered treasures.

sentences.zh: 湣王之遇殺，其子法章變名姓為莒太史敫家庸。

translations.literal: When King Min was killed, his son Fa Zhang changed his name and worked as a servant in the household of the Grand Scribe Jiao of Ju.
translations.idiomatic: After King Min was killed, his son Fa Zhang assumed a false identity and took employment as a servant in the household of the Grand Scribe Jiao of Ju.

sentences.zh: 太史敫女奇法章狀貌，以為非恒人，憐而常竊衣食之，而與私通焉。

translations.literal: The daughter of Grand Scribe Jiao found Fa Zhang's appearance remarkable, believing he was no ordinary man. She pitied him and secretly provided him with clothes and food, and they became secretly intimate.
translations.idiomatic: The daughter of Grand Scribe Jiao was struck by Fa Zhang's remarkable bearing and concluded that he was no ordinary man. She took pity on him, secretly provided him with clothing and food, and the two became lovers.

sentences.zh: 淖齒既以去莒，莒中人及齊亡臣相聚求湣王子，欲立之。

translations.literal: After Nao Chi departed from Ju, the people of Ju and the exiled ministers of Qi gathered together to seek King Min's son, intending to enthrone him.
translations.idiomatic: After Nao Chi departed from Ju, the people of the city and the exiled ministers of Qi gathered together in search of King Min's son, hoping to place him on the throne.

sentences.zh: 法章懼其誅己也，久之，乃敢自言「我湣王子也」。

translations.literal: Fa Zhang feared they might execute him, and only after a long time did he dare to reveal: 'I am King Min's son.'
translations.idiomatic: Fa Zhang feared they might put him to death. Only after some time did he finally dare to come forward and declare: "I am the son of King Min."

sentences.zh: 於是莒人共立法章，是為襄王。

translations.literal: Thereupon the people of Ju together enthroned Fa Zhang, who became King Xiang.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Ju then jointly placed Fa Zhang on the throne, and he became King Xiang.

sentences.zh: 以保莒城而布告齊國中：「王已立在莒矣。」

translations.literal: They defended the city of Ju and issued a proclamation throughout Qi: 'The king has been established at Ju.'
translations.idiomatic: They fortified the city of Ju and issued a proclamation across the state of Qi: "A new king has been established at Ju."

sentences.zh: 襄王既立，立太史氏女為王后，是為君王后，生子建。

translations.literal: After King Xiang was enthroned, he made the daughter of the Grand Scribe his queen. She was known as Queen Junwang, and she bore a son named Jian.
translations.idiomatic: Once King Xiang was established on the throne, he made the daughter of the Grand Scribe his queen. She became known as Queen Junwang, and she bore him a son named Jian.

sentences.zh: 太史敫曰：「女不取媒因自嫁，非吾種也，汙吾世。」

translations.literal: Grand Scribe Jiao said: 'A daughter who does not wait for a matchmaker but marries herself off is not of my lineage and has sullied my family's honor.'
translations.idiomatic: Grand Scribe Jiao declared: "A daughter who marries without a matchmaker's arrangement is no child of mine. She has brought disgrace upon our family."

sentences.zh: 終身不睹君王后。

translations.literal: He refused to see Queen Junwang for the rest of his life.
translations.idiomatic: He refused to see Queen Junwang for the rest of his life.

sentences.zh: 君王后賢，不以不睹故失人子之禮。

translations.literal: Queen Junwang was virtuous and did not, because of his refusal to see her, neglect the proprieties of a daughter.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Junwang was a woman of great virtue. Despite her father's refusal to see her, she never neglected the duties proper to a daughter.

sentences.zh: 襄王在莒五年，田單以即墨攻破燕軍，迎襄王於莒，入臨菑。

translations.literal: King Xiang was at Ju for five years. Tian Dan used Jimo as his base to defeat the Yan army, welcomed King Xiang from Ju, and entered Linzi.
translations.idiomatic: King Xiang remained at Ju for five years. Then Tian Dan, launching his campaign from Jimo, broke through the Yan army, escorted King Xiang from Ju, and entered the capital Linzi.

sentences.zh: 齊故地盡復屬齊。

translations.literal: All of Qi's former territory was restored to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: All of Qi's former territories were restored to its control.

sentences.zh: 齊封田單為安平君。

translations.literal: Qi enfeoffed Tian Dan as Lord Anping.
translations.idiomatic: Qi enfeoffed Tian Dan as Lord Anping.

sentences.zh: 十四年，秦擊我剛壽。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, Qin attacked our Gangshou.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourteenth year, Qin attacked the city of Gangshou.

sentences.zh: 十九年，襄王卒，子建立。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year, King Xiang died. His son Jian succeeded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the nineteenth year, King Xiang died and was succeeded by his son Jian.

sentences.zh: 王建立六年，秦攻趙，齊楚救之。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of King Jian's reign, Qin attacked Zhao. Qi and Chu went to its rescue.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of King Jian's reign, Qin attacked Zhao, and Qi and Chu went to its rescue.

sentences.zh: 秦計曰：「齊楚救趙，親則退兵，不親遂攻之。」

translations.literal: Qin calculated: 'If Qi and Chu truly intend to rescue Zhao, we will withdraw. If they are not committed, we will press the attack.'
translations.idiomatic: Qin assessed the situation: "If Qi and Chu are truly committed to rescuing Zhao, we shall withdraw. If they are not, we shall press the attack."

sentences.zh: 趙無食，請粟於齊，齊不聽。

translations.literal: Zhao had no food and requested grain from Qi, but Qi refused.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao had exhausted its food supplies and appealed to Qi for grain, but Qi refused.

sentences.zh: 周子曰：「不如聽之以退秦兵，不聽則秦兵不卻，是秦之計中而齊楚之計過也。

translations.literal: Zhou Zi said: 'It would be better to agree in order to force Qin to withdraw. If we refuse, Qin's troops will not retreat, which means Qin's strategy succeeds while Qi and Chu's strategy fails.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zi counseled: "Better to provide the grain and thereby compel Qin to withdraw. If we refuse, Qin's troops will not retreat, which means Qin's strategy will succeed and the strategy of Qi and Chu will fail.

sentences.zh: 且趙之於齊楚，捍蔽也，猶齒之有脣也，脣亡則齒寒。

translations.literal: Moreover, Zhao serves as a shield for Qi and Chu, just as teeth have lips — when the lips are gone, the teeth grow cold.
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, Zhao serves as a shield for Qi and Chu, just as the lips protect the teeth. When the lips are gone, the teeth grow cold."

sentences.zh: 今日亡趙，明日患及齊楚。

translations.literal: If Zhao perishes today, the disaster will reach Qi and Chu tomorrow.
translations.idiomatic: "If Zhao perishes today, the disaster will reach Qi and Chu tomorrow."

sentences.zh: 且救趙之務，宜若奉漏甕沃焦釜也。

translations.literal: Moreover, the urgency of rescuing Zhao is like carrying a leaking jar to pour water on a burning pot.
translations.idiomatic: "The urgency of rescuing Zhao is like carrying a leaking jug to douse a scorching cauldron — there is no time to waste."

sentences.zh: 夫救趙，高義也；

translations.literal: Rescuing Zhao is a noble act of righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: "To rescue Zhao is an act of the highest righteousness."

sentences.zh: 卻秦兵，顯名也。

translations.literal: Repelling Qin's armies is a display of illustrious reputation.
translations.idiomatic: "To repel the armies of Qin is to win illustrious renown."

sentences.zh: 義救亡國，威卻彊秦之兵，不務為此而務愛粟，為國計者過矣。」

translations.literal: To righteously rescue a perishing state, to majestically repel the armies of mighty Qin — to neglect this and instead cling to grain, is a grave error for those who plan the affairs of state.
translations.idiomatic: "To righteously rescue a perishing state and majestically repel the armies of mighty Qin — to neglect all this for the sake of hoarding grain is a grave miscalculation by those who direct our affairs of state."

sentences.zh: 齊王弗聽。

translations.literal: The King of Qi did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: But the King of Qi would not listen.

sentences.zh: 秦破趙於長平四十餘萬，遂圍邯鄲。

translations.literal: Qin defeated Zhao at Changping, destroying over four hundred thousand troops, and then besieged Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Qin then annihilated more than four hundred thousand Zhao troops at Changping and proceeded to besiege Handan.

sentences.zh: 十六年，秦滅周。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Qin destroyed Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year, Qin extinguished the Zhou dynasty.

sentences.zh: 君王后卒。

translations.literal: Queen Junwang died.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Junwang died.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，秦置東郡。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year, Qin established the Eastern Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-third year, Qin established the Eastern Commandery.

sentences.zh: 二十八年，王入朝秦，秦王政置酒咸陽。

translations.literal: In the twenty-eighth year, the King went to pay court to Qin. King Zheng of Qin held a banquet at Xianyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-eighth year, King Jian traveled to pay court to Qin. King Zheng of Qin hosted a banquet in his honor at Xianyang.

sentences.zh: 三十五年，秦滅韓。

translations.literal: In the thirty-fifth year, Qin destroyed Han.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirty-fifth year, Qin destroyed Han.

sentences.zh: 三十七年，秦滅趙。

translations.literal: In the thirty-seventh year, Qin destroyed Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirty-seventh year, Qin destroyed Zhao.

sentences.zh: 三十八年，燕使荊軻刺秦王，秦王覺，殺軻。

translations.literal: In the thirty-eighth year, Yan sent Jing Ke to assassinate the King of Qin. The King of Qin detected the plot and killed Ke.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirty-eighth year, Yan sent Jing Ke to assassinate the King of Qin. The king detected the plot and killed Jing Ke.

sentences.zh: 明年，秦破燕，燕王亡走遼東。

translations.literal: The following year, Qin defeated Yan, and the King of Yan fled to Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, Qin defeated Yan, and the King of Yan fled to Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 明年，秦滅魏，秦兵次於歷下。

translations.literal: The following year, Qin destroyed Wei. Qin troops were encamped at Lixia.
translations.idiomatic: The year after that, Qin destroyed Wei and stationed its forces at Lixia.

sentences.zh: 四十二年，秦滅楚。

translations.literal: In the forty-second year, Qin destroyed Chu.
translations.idiomatic: In the forty-second year, Qin destroyed Chu.

sentences.zh: 明年，虜代王嘉，滅燕王喜。

translations.literal: The following year, they captured King Jia of Dai and destroyed King Xi of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, Qin captured King Jia of Dai and destroyed King Xi of Yan.

sentences.zh: 四十四年，秦兵擊齊。

translations.literal: In the forty-fourth year, Qin's forces attacked Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the forty-fourth year, the forces of Qin attacked Qi.

sentences.zh: 齊王聽相后勝計，不戰，以兵降秦。

translations.literal: The King of Qi followed the plan of his chief minister Hou Sheng, did not fight, and surrendered his army to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qi heeded the counsel of his chief minister Hou Sheng. Without offering battle, he surrendered his forces to Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦虜王建，遷之共。

translations.literal: Qin took King Jian prisoner and relocated him to Gong.
translations.idiomatic: Qin took King Jian prisoner and exiled him to Gong.

sentences.zh: 遂滅齊為郡。

translations.literal: Thus Qi was destroyed and made into commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the state of Qi was extinguished and absorbed into Qin's commanderies.

sentences.zh: 天下壹并於秦，秦王政立號為皇帝。

translations.literal: All under Heaven was united under Qin. King Zheng of Qin established the title of Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: All under Heaven was united under Qin, and King Zheng of Qin proclaimed himself Emperor.

sentences.zh: 始，君王后賢，事秦謹，與諸侯信，齊亦東邊海上，秦日夜攻三晉、燕、楚，五國各自救於秦，以故王建立四十餘年不受兵。

translations.literal: Initially, Queen Junwang was wise. She was cautious in dealings with Qin, maintained trust with the feudal lords, and Qi was situated in the east along the seacoast. Qin attacked the Three Jin, Yan, and Chu day and night, and the five states were each preoccupied with defending themselves against Qin. For this reason, King Jian reigned more than forty years without suffering attack.
translations.idiomatic: In the beginning, Queen Junwang had been a wise ruler. She managed relations with Qin with great care, maintained trust with the other feudal lords, and Qi was shielded by its position on the eastern seaboard. While Qin relentlessly attacked the Three Jin states, Yan, and Chu, those five states were each consumed with their own defense against Qin. For this reason, King Jian reigned for more than forty years without suffering a military attack.

sentences.zh: 君王后死，后勝相齊，多受秦閒金，多使賓客入秦，秦又多予金，客皆為反閒，勸王去從朝秦，不修攻戰之備，不助五國攻秦，秦以故得滅五國。

translations.literal: After Queen Junwang died, Hou Sheng became chief minister of Qi. He accepted much gold from Qin's spies, sent many guests to Qin, and Qin in turn gave them much gold. The guests all became double agents, persuading the king to abandon the alliance and pay court to Qin, to neglect military preparations, and to refuse to help the five states attack Qin. Qin was thus able to destroy the five states.
translations.idiomatic: After Queen Junwang's death, Hou Sheng became chief minister of Qi. He accepted bribes of gold from Qin's agents and sent numerous envoys to Qin, where Qin in turn lavished them with gold. These envoys all became double agents, persuading the king to abandon the vertical alliance, pay tribute to Qin, neglect all military preparations, and refuse to aid the five states in attacking Qin. Qin was thereby able to destroy all five states.

sentences.zh: 五國已亡，秦兵卒入臨淄，民莫敢格者。

translations.literal: After the five states had perished, Qin's soldiers finally entered Linzi. None of the people dared resist.
translations.idiomatic: Once the five states had been destroyed, Qin's soldiers at last marched into Linzi. Not a single citizen dared to resist.

sentences.zh: 王建遂降，遷於共。

translations.literal: King Jian then surrendered and was relocated to Gong.
translations.idiomatic: King Jian surrendered and was exiled to Gong.

sentences.zh: 故齊人怨王建不蚤與諸侯合從攻秦，聽姦臣賓客以亡其國，歌之曰：「松耶柏耶？

translations.literal: The people of Qi thus resented King Jian for not joining the feudal lords early in a vertical alliance to attack Qin, and for heeding treacherous ministers and guests to the ruin of his state. They sang: 'Pine trees, cypress trees?'
translations.idiomatic: The people of Qi therefore blamed King Jian for failing to join the feudal lords in a vertical alliance against Qin while there was still time, and for heeding treacherous ministers and foreign agents until his state was lost. They sang a lament: "Were they pines or cypresses?

sentences.zh: 住建共者客耶？」

translations.literal: Those who sent Jian to dwell in Gong — were they his guests?
translations.idiomatic: Those who sent Jian to dwell in Gong — were they not his own guests?"

sentences.zh: 疾建用客之不詳也。

translations.literal: They resented Jian for employing guests unwisely.
translations.idiomatic: They condemned King Jian for his reckless trust in foreign advisors.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：蓋孔子晚而喜易。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: It is said that Confucius in his later years delighted in the Book of Changes.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: It is said that Confucius in his later years came to delight in the Book of Changes.

sentences.zh: 易之為術，幽明遠矣，非通人達才孰能注意焉！

translations.literal: The art of the Changes is profound and far-reaching. Who but a person of broad learning and penetrating ability could pay it proper attention!
translations.idiomatic: The art of the Book of Changes is profound and far-reaching in its illumination. Who but a person of broad learning and deep insight could give it the attention it deserves!

sentences.zh: 笔周太史之卦田敬仲完，占至十世之後；

translations.literal: When the Grand Astrologer of Zhou cast a hexagram for Tian Jingzhong Wan, the divination reached ten generations into the future.
translations.idiomatic: When the Grand Astrologer of Zhou cast a hexagram for Tian Jingzhong Wan, the prophecy reached ten generations into the future.

sentences.zh: 及完奔齊，懿仲卜之亦云。

translations.literal: And when Wan fled to Qi, Yi Zhong's divination also said the same.
translations.idiomatic: And when Wan fled to Qi, Yi Zhong's divination foretold the very same outcome.

sentences.zh: 田乞及常所以比犯二君，專齊國之政，非必事勢之漸然也，蓋若遵厭兆祥云。

translations.literal: The reason Tian Qi and Tian Chang successively offended two rulers and monopolized the government of Qi was not necessarily due to the gradual trend of events. Perhaps they were following the fulfillment of the portents and omens.
translations.idiomatic: That Tian Qi and Tian Chang successively defied two rulers and seized sole control of Qi's government was not simply the result of a gradual trend in human affairs. Perhaps they were carried along by the inexorable fulfillment of the very portents and omens that had been foretold.
